etymology.description: semantic 辵(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") + phonetic 睘(睘) (qióng) -> to go back; to return
etymology.interpretation: The character 還(還) (huán, "to return") combines the semantic element 辵(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") indicating movement, and the phonetic element 睘(睘) (qióng), providing the sound. The notion is of moving back or returning.
etymology.simplification: The traditional 還 was simplified to 还 by reducing the complex 辵(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") component to simpler form 辶.

examples.chinese: 我还在工作(我還在工作)。
他已经吃过午饭，但他还饿(他已經吃過午飯，但他還餓)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǐjīng chī guò wǔfàn, dàn tā hái è.
examples.english: He already had lunch, but he is still hungry.

examples.chinese: 虽然已经很晚了，我们还得继续工作(雖然已經很晚了，我們還得繼續工作)。
examples.pinyin: Suīrán yǐjīng hěn wǎn le, wǒmen hái děi jìxù gōngzuò.
examples.english: Although it's already very late, we still have to continue working.
